author: Dr. Lizard
title: How does C5 blockade prevent thrombosis in paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria?
answer: C5 inhibition prevents MAC formation, reducing intravascular hemolysis, free hemoglobin release, and nitric oxide depletion.
detail: Blocking MAC assembly stops red cell lysis, eliminating the source of free hemoglobin that scavenges nitric oxide.
Preserved nitric oxide maintains vasodilation and inhibits platelet activation, reducing thrombotic risk by approximately 85 percent.
C5a inhibition prevents complement-mediated activation of coagulation cascade and suppression of fibrinolysis.
Eculizumab reduced thrombosis from 50 percent of deaths to rare events, dramatically improving long-term survival.
releaseNote: C5 blockade prevents thrombosis in paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria by inhibiting MAC formation, preserving nitric oxide, and reducing thrombotic risk.
